---
title: "FIN 452 QTP"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
set.seed(123)
library(factoextra)
library(tidyquant)
library(tidyverse)
library(PerformanceAnalytics)
library(cluster)
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
# Function to calculate financial metrics for a given dataframe of stock prices
calculateStatistics <- function(price_data) {
  # Compute daily log returns
  returns_df <- price_data %>%
    group_by(symbol) %>%
    tq_transmute(select     = adjusted,
                 mutate_fun = periodReturn,
                 period     = "daily",
                 type       = "arithmetic",
                 col_rename = "daily_return") %>%
    ungroup()

  # Identify benchmark (SPY) returns for beta calculation
  market_returns <- returns_df %>%
    filter(symbol == "SPY") %>%
    select(date, market_return = daily_return)

  # Join market returns back to the main returns dataframe
  returns_with_market <- returns_df %>%
    left_join(market_returns, by = "date")

  # Compute metrics per ticker
  # Metrics include: average return, volatility, sharpe ratio, skewness, kurtosis, and asset beta
  summary_stats <- returns_with_market %>%
    filter(symbol != "SPY") %>% 
    group_by(symbol) %>%
    summarise(
      avg_return   = mean(daily_return, na.rm = TRUE),
      volatility   = sd(daily_return, na.rm = TRUE),
      sharpe_ratio = if(sd(daily_return, na.rm = TRUE) != 0) mean(daily_return, na.rm = TRUE) / sd(daily_return, na.rm = TRUE) else NA,
      skewness     = PerformanceAnalytics::skewness(daily_return),
      kurtosis     = PerformanceAnalytics::kurtosis(daily_return),
      asset_beta   = cov(daily_return, market_return, use = "complete.obs") / var(market_return, na.rm = TRUE)
    )

  return(summary_stats)
}
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
# Example usage with the tickers previously requested
tickers <- c("SPY", "XLB", "XLC", "XLE", "XLF", "XLI", "XLK", "XLP", "XLRE", "XLV", "XLU", "XLY")

# Pull historical data
historical_prices <- tickers %>%
  tq_get(get = "stock.prices",
         from = "2000-01-01",
         to = "2024-12-31") %>%
  group_by(symbol) %>%
  mutate(
    # Open-to-Close: Return within the trading day
    open_close_return = (close / open) - 1,
    # Close-to-Close: Total daily return
    close_close_return = close / lag(close) - 1,
    # Close-to-Open: Overnight return (Previous Close to Current Open)
    close_open_return = (open / lag(close)) - 1
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Results are stored in the ticker_metrics_df
ticker_metrics_df <- calculateStatistics(historical_prices)

scaled_metrics <- ticker_metrics_df %>% 
  select(avg_return, volatility, sharpe_ratio, asset_beta) %>% 
  scale()

max_k <- nrow(scaled_metrics) - 1

wss <- map_dbl(1:max_k, function(k){
  kmeans(scaled_metrics, centers = k, nstart = 25)$tot.withinss
})
tibble(k = 1:max_k, wss = wss) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = k, y = wss)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue", linewidth = 1) + geom_point() + scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:max_k)
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
#Compute Clusters Using K means
kmeans_res <- kmeans(scaled_metrics, centers = 3, nstart = 25)

ticker_metrics_df <- ticker_metrics_df %>% 
  mutate(cluster_kmeans = as.factor(kmeans_res$cluster))
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
#Compute clusters using heirarchical clustering
dist_mat <- dist(scaled_metrics, method = "euclidean")
cluster_result <- hclust(dist_mat, method = "ward.D2")
cluster_result$labels <- ticker_metrics_df$symbol

fviz_dend(cluster_result, k = 3, cex = 0.8, main = "Hierarchical Clustering of Assets", horiz = TRUE, rect = TRUE)
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
ticker_metrics_df <- ticker_metrics_df %>% 
  mutate(cluster_hclust = as.factor(cutree(cluster_result, k = 3)))

comparison_df <- ticker_metrics_df %>% 
  select(symbol, cluster_kmeans, cluster_hclust) %>% 
  arrange(cluster_kmeans)


sil_kmean <- silhouette(kmeans_res$cluster, dist(scaled_metrics))

fviz_silhouette(sil_kmean)

sil_heir <- silhouette(cutree(cluster_result, k = 3), dist_mat)

fviz_silhouette(sil_heir)

cluster_profiles <- ticker_metrics_df %>% 
  group_by(cluster_kmeans) %>% 
  summarise(
    avg_beta = mean(asset_beta),
    avg_return = mean(avg_return),
    avg_vol = mean(volatility),
    avg_sharpe = mean(sharpe_ratio),
    n_assets = n()
  ) %>% 
  arrange(desc(avg_beta))


```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
#Assign descriptive names to clusters in comparison_df
comparison_df <- comparison_df %>%
mutate(cluster_name = case_match(cluster_kmeans,
"1" ~ "High Growth",
"2" ~ "Conservative",
"3" ~ "Market Performers",
.default = "Other"                              
))                                              
# Apply cluster descriptors to historical_prices
historical_prices <- historical_prices %>%
  left_join(comparison_df %>% select(symbol, cluster_name), by = "symbol")
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
# Function to denoise prices using a Kalman Filter (Local Level Model)
kalman_denoise <- function(x, Q = 0.0001, R = 0.01) {
  # Q: Process noise (how much we expect the 'true' price to move)
  # R: Measurement noise (how much market jitter we want to filter out)

  n <- length(x)
  x_hat <- numeric(n) # Filtered state estimates
  P <- numeric(n)     # Error covariance

  # Initialization
  x_hat[1] <- x[1]
  P[1] <- 1.0

  for (t in 2:n) {
    # 1. Predict Step
    x_hat_minus <- x_hat[t-1]
    P_minus <- P[t-1] + Q

    # 2. Update Step (Correction)
    K <- P_minus / (P_minus + R) # Kalman Gain
    x_hat[t] <- x_hat_minus + K * (x[t] - x_hat_minus)
    P[t] <- (1 - K) * P_minus
  }
  return(x_hat)
}

# Apply the Kalman Filter to denoise each ticker's adjusted price
historical_prices <- historical_prices %>%
  group_by(symbol) %>%
  mutate(denoised_close = kalman_denoise(adjusted)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Visualize the denoising effect for a sample ticker (e.g., SPY)
historical_prices %>%
  filter(symbol == "SPY", date >= "2024-01-01") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = adjusted, color = "Raw Adjusted"), alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_line(aes(y = denoised_close, color = "Denoised (Kalman)"), size = 1) +
  theme_tq() +
  labs(title = "Kalman Filter Denoising: SPY",
       y = "Price", color = "Series") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Raw Adjusted" = "grey50", "Denoised (Kalman)" = "blue"))
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
# Remove SPY and create a new dataframe for portfolio analysis
historical_prices_no_spy <- historical_prices %>%
  filter(symbol != "SPY")

# Function to backtest a portfolio with monthly rebalancing
backtestPortfolio <- function(data, cluster_weights, initial_investment) {
  # cluster_weights: named vector e.g., c("High Growth" = 0.4, "Conservative" = 0.3, "Market Performers" = 0.3)
  
  # Identify rebalance dates (first trading day of each month)
  rebalance_dates <- data %>%
    mutate(month_year = floor_date(date, "month")) %>%
    group_by(month_year) %>%
    filter(date == min(date)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    pull(date) %>%
    unique() %>%
    sort()
  
  # Initialize portfolio tracking
  portfolio_history <- list()
  current_shares <- data.frame(symbol = unique(data$symbol), shares = 0)
  
  # Loop through rebalance dates
  for (i in seq_along(rebalance_dates)) {
    current_date <- rebalance_dates[i]
    
    # Get prices for today, only keeping tickers with valid prices
    today_prices <- data %>%
      filter(date == current_date, !is.na(adjusted)) %>%
      select(symbol, cluster_name, adjusted)
    
    # Dynamically calculate cluster counts for this specific date
    current_cluster_counts <- today_prices %>%
      count(cluster_name)
    
    if (i == 1) {
      # Initial investment allocation
      total_value <- initial_investment
    } else {
      # Calculate current value based on previous shares
      total_value <- today_prices %>%
        left_join(current_shares, by = "symbol") %>%
        mutate(value = shares * adjusted) %>%
        summarise(total = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
        pull(total)
    }
    
    # Target allocations by cluster
    targets <- today_prices %>%
      left_join(current_cluster_counts, by = "cluster_name") %>%
      mutate(
        target_cluster_val = total_value * cluster_weights[cluster_name],
        target_ticker_val = target_cluster_val / n,
        new_shares = target_ticker_val / adjusted,
        old_shares = current_shares$shares[match(symbol, current_shares$symbol)],
        # Set old_shares to 0 if it was NA (newly available ticker)
        old_shares = ifelse(is.na(old_shares), 0, old_shares),
        shares_bought_sold = new_shares - old_shares
      )
    
    # Update current shares
    current_shares$shares <- targets$new_shares[match(current_shares$symbol, targets$symbol)]
    
    # Record history
    portfolio_history[[i]] <- targets %>%
      mutate(date = current_date, total_portfolio_value = total_value)
  }
  
  # Combine history into a single dataframe
  final_history <- bind_rows(portfolio_history)
  
  # Pivot to wide format as requested
  wide_history <- final_history %>%
    select(date, symbol, new_shares, total_portfolio_value) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = symbol, values_from = new_shares) %>%
    arrange(date)
    
  return(wide_history)
}

# Test function
weights <- c("High Growth" = 0.4, "Conservative" = 0.3, "Market Performers" = 0.3)
test <- backtestPortfolio(historical_prices_no_spy, weights, 100000)
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
# Simple Moving Average Trading Strategy
# Par 1: 30-day SMA of denoised_close
# Par 2: 100-day SMA of denoised_close
# Signal: 1 if Par 1 > Par 2, -1 if Par 1 < Par 2

historical_prices_no_spy <- historical_prices_no_spy %>%
  group_by(symbol) %>%
  mutate(
    par1 = TTR::SMA(denoised_close, n = 30),
    par2 = TTR::SMA(denoised_close, n = 100),
    signal = case_when(
      par1 > par2 ~ 1,
      par1 < par2 ~ -1,
      .default = NA_real_
    ),
    # Current position: start with 1 (long) and follow signal when available
    pos = if_else(is.na(signal), 1, signal),
    # Returns of existing positions
    return_exist = if_else(pos == lag(pos, default = 1),close_close_return * pos, 0),
    # Return on portfolio changes (rebalancing at the open)
    new_returns = if_else(pos != lag(pos, default = 1), pos * open_close_return, 0),
    # Total return
    total_ret = return_exist + new_returns,
    # Cumulative returns
    cum_ret = cumprod(1 + coalesce(total_ret, 0))
  ) %>%
  group_by(symbol, month = floor_date(date, "month")) %>%
  mutate(cum_ret_monthly = cumprod(1 + coalesce(total_ret, 0))) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-month)

# View results for a sample ticker
historical_prices_no_spy %>% 
  filter(symbol == "XLK") %>% 
  select(date, symbol, denoised_close, signal, pos, total_ret, cum_ret, cum_ret_monthly) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(signal)) %>% 
  head(10)
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
# Integrated Backtesting: Monthly Rebalancing with Daily SMA Long/Short Strategy
# This merges the signal logic into the portfolio value tracking

backtestStrategyPortfolio <- function(data, cluster_weights, initial_investment) {
  # 1. Identify rebalance dates (first trading day of each month)
  rebalance_dates <- data %>%
    mutate(month_year = floor_date(date, "month")) %>%
    group_by(month_year) %>%
    filter(date == min(date)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    pull(date) %>%
    unique() %>%
    sort()
  
  all_dates <- sort(unique(data$date))
  all_symbols <- unique(data$symbol)
  
  daily_portfolio <- data.frame(date = all_dates, total_value = NA_real_)
  
  # ticker_values: dollar value of each ticker's position contribution
  ticker_values <- setNames(rep(0, length(all_symbols)), all_symbols)
  # ticker_pos: direction (1 for long, -1 for short)
  ticker_pos <- setNames(rep(1, length(all_symbols)), all_symbols)
  
  current_total_val <- initial_investment
  
  for (i in seq_along(all_dates)) {
    today <- all_dates[i]
    today_data <- data[data$date == today, ]
    
    if (i == 1) {
      # Initialize at open of first day
      available <- today_data[!is.na(today_data$open) & !is.na(today_data$cluster_name) & !is.na(today_data$pos), ]
      counts <- table(available$cluster_name)
      if (nrow(available) > 0) {
        weights_vec <- cluster_weights[available$cluster_name]
        counts_vec <- as.numeric(counts[available$cluster_name])
        ticker_values[available$symbol] <- (initial_investment * weights_vec) / counts_vec
        ticker_pos[available$symbol] <- available$pos
      }
    } else {
      # A. Overnight performance (yesterday's positions)
      ret_co <- today_data$close_open_return[match(names(ticker_values), today_data$symbol)]
      ticker_values <- ticker_values * (1 + ticker_pos * coalesce(ret_co, 0))
      current_total_val <- sum(ticker_values)
      
      # B. Monthly Rebalance OR Daily Position Update
      if (today %in% rebalance_dates) {
        # Dynamically identify available tickers today
        available <- today_data[!is.na(today_data$open) & !is.na(today_data$cluster_name) & !is.na(today_data$pos), ]
        counts <- table(available$cluster_name)
        ticker_values[] <- 0 # Exit old positions
        if (nrow(available) > 0) {
          weights_vec <- cluster_weights[available$cluster_name]
          counts_vec <- as.numeric(counts[available$cluster_name])
          ticker_values[available$symbol] <- (current_total_val * weights_vec) / counts_vec
          ticker_pos[available$symbol] <- available$pos
        }
      } else {
        # Switch direction if signal changed
        new_pos <- today_data$pos[match(names(ticker_pos), today_data$symbol)]
        update_mask <- !is.na(new_pos) & ticker_values != 0
        ticker_pos[update_mask] <- new_pos[update_mask]
      }
    }
    
    # C. Intraday performance (today's updated positions)
    ret_oc <- today_data$open_close_return[match(names(ticker_values), today_data$symbol)]
    ticker_values <- ticker_values * (1 + ticker_pos * coalesce(ret_oc, 0))
    current_total_val <- sum(ticker_values)
    
    daily_portfolio$total_value[i] <- current_total_val
  }
  
  # Calculate Returns
  daily_portfolio <- daily_portfolio %>%
    mutate(
      daily_ret = total_value / lag(total_value) - 1,
      cum_ret = cumprod(1 + coalesce(daily_ret, 0))
    )
  
  return(daily_portfolio)
}

# Run the integrated backtest
weights <- c("High Growth" = 0.4, "Conservative" = 0.3, "Market Performers" = 0.3)
strategy_backtest <- backtestStrategyPortfolio(historical_prices_no_spy, weights, 100000)

# Plot Cumulative Returns
ggplot(strategy_backtest, aes(x = date, y = cum_ret)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkgreen") +
  theme_tq() +
  labs(title = "Integrated Portfolio Performance: SMA Strategy + Monthly Rebalancing",
       subtitle = "Accounting for Long/Short Positions per Asset",
       y = "Cumulative Return", x = "Date")
```

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
# Optimization: Grid Search for Optimal Cluster Weights
# We vary weights for the three clusters to maximize the Annualized Sharpe Ratio

# 1. Define the grid of weights (summing to 1)
weight_grid <- expand.grid(
  High_Growth = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
  Conservative = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)
) %>%
  mutate(Market_Performers = 1 - (High_Growth + Conservative)) %>%
  filter(Market_Performers >= 0)

# 2. Iterate through the grid and calculate Sharpe Ratio
# Note: This might take a moment depending on the grid density
optimization_results <- weight_grid %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    sharpe_ratio = {
      w_vec <- c("High Growth" = High_Growth, 
                 "Conservative" = Conservative, 
                 "Market Performers" = Market_Performers)
      
      # Run backtest for these weights
      bt_res <- backtestStrategyPortfolio(historical_prices_no_spy, w_vec, 100000)
      
      # Calculate Annualized Sharpe Ratio
      # (Mean daily return / SD daily return) * sqrt(252)
      if (all(is.na(bt_res$daily_ret))) {
        NA_real_
      } else {
        mean(bt_res$daily_ret, na.rm = TRUE) / sd(bt_res$daily_ret, na.rm = TRUE) * sqrt(252)
      }
    }
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 3. Identify the best weight combination
best_weights <- optimization_results %>%
  arrange(desc(sharpe_ratio)) %>%
  head(1)

print("Optimal Weights Found:")
print(best_weights)

# 4. Plot the optimization surface
ggplot(optimization_results, aes(x = High_Growth, y = Conservative, fill = sharpe_ratio)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  labs(title = "Cluster Weight Optimization Surface",
       subtitle = "Maximizing Annualized Sharpe Ratio",
       x = "Weight: High Growth",
       y = "Weight: Conservative",
       fill = "Sharpe Ratio") +
  theme_minimal()
```
